app.name: Moku
app.one_liner: A fast, minimal manga reader frontend for Suwayomi-Server built with Tauri and Svelte 5.

screens.name: Downloads
screens.purpose: Monitor and manage chapter download queue with retry and priority controls.

screens.name: Library
screens.purpose: Display manga organized into folders with stats, unread counts, and filtering by publication status.

screens.name: Reader
screens.purpose: Read manga with configurable fit modes, keybinds, zoom, pan, and page markers.

screens.name: Search
screens.purpose: Discover manga via keyword or tag-based search across sources.

screens.name: Series Detail
screens.purpose: View manga metadata, manage automation rules, and access markers.

screens.name: Settings
screens.purpose: Configure app-wide preferences including theme, reader defaults, and extension management.

rust_commands.name: apply_system_theme
rust_commands.purpose: Switch app theme based on system preference.

rust_commands.name: open_in_file_manager
rust_commands.purpose: Open the local directory containing downloaded manga chapters.

rust_commands.name: retry_download
rust_commands.purpose: Retry a failed chapter download with enhanced toast notification.

rust_commands.name: sync_tracking_progress
rust_commands.purpose: Perform two-way synchronization of reading progress between reader and app.

rust_commands.name: toggle_library_display_option
rust_commands.purpose: Update UI visibility of card stats or folder visibility in library view.

mvp_plan: week 1: Add marker creation and display on progress bar
week 1: Implement core library view with folder-based organization
week 1: Integrate basic reader with zoom and navigation
week 2: Add per-folder sorting and filtering
week 2: Build download queue with retry logic
week 3: Add Discord Rich Presence support
week 3: Develop automation panel for pre-download and cleanup
acceptance_tests: When download fails, retry button appears and successfully requeues the chapter.
When system theme changes, app UI switches automatically without restart.
When tracking poll runs, reading progress updates correctly in both reader and series detail.
When user toggles 'Hide Completed Manga', completed titles disappear from library view.
